Projects
1. MXene based Functional Nanocomposite Nasal Sensor for the Identification of Possible Diseases/Abnormalities, Internal SEED Grant: VIT AP/SPORIC/RGEMS/ 22-23/016(Ongoing)
2. Fabrication and Characterization of Solution Processed Nanosheets and Nanocomposites based Resistive thin Films for Heating elements as well as Sensing elements applications (Smart Temperature, Strain, Tactile, Pressure, Force, Capacitive, Robotics, Wearable Body Warmers etc.).
Patents
1. Nagarjuna Neella, Venkateswarlu Gaddam, M. M. Nayak, N. S. Dinesh and K. Rajanna, “Reduced Graphene Oxide-Silver Nanocomposite Films for Temperature Sensor Application”, USA Patent Filed, File No: USA 16/090/737, International Patent–PCT Filed, File No: PCT/IB2017/052017 and Indian Patent Filed, File No: 201641012543.
2. Vaishakh Kedambaimoole, Pavithra B Jain, Manasa, Nagarjuna Neella, M. M. Nayak, K. Rajanna and N. S. Dinesh, “Highly Sensitive Reduced Graphene Oxide-Nickel Composite based Cryogenic Temperature Sensor” USA Patent Filed, File No: USA 16/520/449 and Indian Patent Filed, File No: 201841027819.
3. Nagarjuna Neella, Vaishakh Kedambaimoole, M. M. Nayak, N. S. Dinesh and K. Rajanna, “Reduced Graphene Oxide Nanomaterial Coated Cotton Fabric as a Heater Device and Method Therefore”, International Patent–PCT Filed, File No: PCT/IB2018/050721 and Indian patent Filed, File No: 201741004725.
4. Nagarjuna Neella, Venkateswarlu Gaddam, Veera Pandi N, K. Rajanna, M. M. Nayak, N. S. Dinesh, “Graphene Oxide–Platinum Nanocomposite for Sensor Applications”, Indian Patent Filed, File No: 201641031595.
5. Nagarjuna Neella, Manjunath. M.S, M. M. Nayak and K. Rajanna, “Reduced Graphene Oxide Nanosheets based Strain Gauges for Pressure Transducers and Method Therefore”, Indian Patent Filed, File No: 201741007404.
6. Vijay Shirhatti, Vaishakh Kedambaimoole, Suresh Nuthalapati, Nagarjuna Neella, M. M. Nayak and K. Rajanna, “Capacitive Sensor and Process of Preparation Thereof”, Indian Patent Filed, File No: 201841013832."
Awards
Received Gandhian Young Technological Innovation Awards 2018 (GYTI Awards 2018) for the project entitled: “Development of Graphene Coated Conductive Fabrics based Smart Wearable Body Warmers for Defense and Medical Applications”, from The President of India, Rashtrapati Bhavan, New Delhi, 2018 during 19-23rd March 2018, Festival of Innovations and Entrepreneurship 2018 (FINE 2018), India.
Received RULA International awards 2019 (RULA Awards 2019) for the project entitled: “Al:ZnO Nanosheets on Flexible Stainless Steel Substrate as Impact Sensor”, from The Director, IJRULA and RULA Awards, World Research Council and United Medical Council, 26th February 2019 at Grandeur Hall, Breeze Residency, Trichy, Tamilnadu, India.

Organizing and technical committee member for various conferences conducted at India, U.S.A, Singapore, China
Motivated students to present their research work at various national project expos, Smart India Hackathon, conducted by industry and academic institutes
